title: Basic Email Security

jokes.text: Computer no worky
jokes.setup: Dean needs tech help with a computer problem
jokes.punchline: Uses childish baby talk instead of proper description
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for the Dean - his tendency to be inappropriately childish in serious situations. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Pelton, I can't keep track of all the parakeets you had growing up
jokes.setup: Elroy assumes the Dean forgot his password again
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurdly specific knowledge about the Dean's childhood pet naming conventions
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific detail that feels very Community - the absurd specificity of knowing someone's childhood pet history

jokes.text: You have been owned by Elite Fleet 69. Our hacks are all up in your systems. I'm not bothering to pronounce them, but there are a lot of z's being used.
jokes.setup: Reading the hacker message aloud
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the stereotypical 'hacker speak' with excessive z's without actually doing it
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on hacker clichés while avoiding the cliché itself - very Community's self-aware style

jokes.text: Is there another Mask movie coming out?
jokes.setup: Someone says 'Oh, crap' about the hacking
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets the concern as being about a sequel to The Mask
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke - functional but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: No parlo hablo too late at Greendale Elroy
jokes.setup: Elroy says it's too late to stop the hack
jokes.punchline: Dean's mangled attempt at Spanish mixed with his Greendale optimism
jokes.explanation: Pure Dean Pelton - his inappropriate optimism and butchered Spanish perfectly in character

jokes.text: You could cut the hardline at the mainframe. Hardline. Mainframe.
jokes.setup: Elroy suggests a technical solution
jokes.punchline: Repeats the tech jargon words, highlighting how generic they sound
jokes.explanation: Elroy's characteristic awareness of how ridiculous things sound - meta but character-appropriate

jokes.text: To the mainframe! To the mainframe!
jokes.setup: Elroy's tech jargon suggestion
jokes.punchline: Everyone gets excited and starts chanting meaningless tech terms like a battle cry
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from tech talk to war cry - captures Community's tendency toward sudden group dynamics

jokes.text: You were being sarcastic? Yeah, but you should look at this.
jokes.setup: Everyone got excited about the mainframe plan
jokes.punchline: Reveals Elroy was being sarcastic but then immediately finds something actually important
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - deflates the absurd moment then immediately redirects to plot. Classic Elroy deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Don't click it. Oh, what, the green skull will give me a virus?
jokes.setup: Warning not to click a suspicious link
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic dismissal of an obviously dangerous-looking skull icon
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic moment about obvious computer dangers - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Which lunch lady? The hot one? You mean the one who serves the hot food? Mmm, that's the one.
jokes.setup: Discussion about which lunch lady's emails were leaked
jokes.punchline: Clarifies 'hot' lunch lady means the one serving hot food, then confirms that's still the attractive one
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay on 'hot' - works but isn't particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: No one here could ever do that, not even in shifts, it's decades of data.
jokes.setup: Accusation that they're reading the leaked emails
jokes.punchline: Denies it by emphasizing the absurd volume - 'decades' worth
jokes.explanation: Logical escalation to emphasize the impossibility while revealing they probably are reading them

jokes.text: I found a torrent of all the highlights if you don't want to wait for the ads to load
jokes.setup: Discussion about reading leaked email highlights
jokes.punchline: Abed matter-of-factly mentions finding an illegal torrent for convenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - his technical efficiency and moral blind spots around digital piracy

jokes.text: Not to get too liberal, but once they're online, does anyone really own the lunch lady's emails?
jokes.setup: Discussing the ethics of reading leaked emails
jokes.punchline: Prefaces a privacy-violating stance as 'liberal' when it's actually the opposite
jokes.explanation: Smart irony about how people misuse political labels to justify questionable behavior

jokes.text: WazzleDazzle.com has got five Bacardi cocktails inspired by the lunch lady's most embarrassing secrets
jokes.setup: Discussion of media coverage of the email leak
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific corporate tie-in exploiting someone's private humiliation
jokes.explanation: Great satirical escalation of how media and brands exploit privacy violations for content

jokes.text: No, just gonna keep the cop you've known for five years at arms length? I get it.
jokes.setup: Police officer arrives to help with the hacking
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive complaint about being treated distantly despite long history
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the cop's insecurity about his relationship with the group

jokes.text: Officer Warburton. I want McDonald's. Wants. McDonald's.
jokes.setup: Mention of the cyber-crime division being new
jokes.punchline: Reveals the cyber-crime specialist is literally a child who only wants McDonald's
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist visual - child in police uniform as 'cyber expert' perfectly satirizes incompetent institutions

jokes.text: It's literally a child in an adult sized police uniform.
jokes.setup: The child cyber-crime officer
jokes.punchline: Deadpan acknowledgment of the absurdity while being helpless about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up that doubles down on the absurdity while showing institutional helplessness

jokes.text: $700. Oh my God, Dean, he's renowned for his racist act. How can he be racist? Listen to his name.
jokes.setup: Revelation they booked controversial comedian Gupta Gupti Gupta
jokes.punchline: Dean's naive assumption that having an ethnic name prevents racism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean logic - his well-meaning but completely wrong understanding of how prejudice works

jokes.text: Though I'm just a cop, born in a small town, raised in the heartland, but I say cancel the performance, and give the government the sweeping powers it needs to detect and eliminate people before they turn into hackers.
jokes.setup: Cop giving his opinion on the situation
jokes.punchline: Escalates from folksy appeal to advocating for authoritarian pre-crime elimination
jokes.explanation: Dark satirical escalation from folksy cop to fascist - excellent structure building to disturbing conclusion

jokes.text: I hear Marvel got really hands on this time, they really pinned in Joss Whedon creatively, so how can that go wrong?
jokes.setup: Cop making small talk about Avengers movie
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically suggests corporate interference with creative control will work out fine
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about Hollywood that was probably topical when aired - functional but not character-specific

jokes.text: Well the cops say that your car trunk has something to do with 9/11. They're not philosophers, they're crossing guards with guns.
jokes.setup: Response to 'the cop says no'
jokes.punchline: Dismisses police authority with absurd example and reductive description
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - absurd example followed by perfect reductive description. 'Crossing guards with guns' is highly quotable

jokes.text: The eyes of the world... Are within hundreds of miles of this room.
jokes.setup: Trying to inspire with 'eyes of the world' rhetoric
jokes.punchline: Deflates immediately by specifying the incredibly modest geographic range
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and deflation - sets up grand rhetoric then immediately undercuts with technical accuracy

jokes.text: Aren't you an atheist? Don't you hate this government? Are you even planning on going to see this comic? Yes, pretty much and of course not, I was planning to protest him.
jokes.setup: Jeff challenging Britta's motivations
jokes.punchline: Britta admitting she planned to protest the very thing she's now defending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta contradiction - her principles override her personal feelings.

jokes.text: We prefer to be called people without color or vaginas.
jokes.setup: Comment about rights being 'For white people. With penises.'
jokes.punchline: Reframes the demographic description in awkwardly clinical negative terms
jokes.explanation: Clever reframing that highlights absurdity of default demographics through negative description

jokes.text: We're the colony so high maintenance the British gave up.
jokes.setup: Britta's speech about American exceptionalism
jokes.punchline: Reframing Revolutionary War as Britain being fed up with difficult colonists
jokes.explanation: Brilliant recontextualizing of American Revolution - 'high maintenance' perfectly captures both colonial demands and modern American entitlement

jokes.text: We're the colony so high maintenance the British gave up. We're so obnoxious the French sent us a statue.
jokes.setup: Britta making patriotic speech about American character
jokes.punchline: Reframes historical events as other countries being annoyed by Americans
jokes.explanation: Excellent parallel structure with clever historical reframing. 'So obnoxious the French sent us a statue' is brilliantly quotable

jokes.text: yes, if our votes had any real power they'd be illegal. Britta! Freedom of speech acknowledged. Trust us.
jokes.setup: Britta's increasingly cynical political rant
jokes.punchline: Dark observation about voting followed by someone cutting her off
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta - her tendency to get too dark and political, with the group's practiced response to shut her down

jokes.text: Britta! Freedom of speech acknowledged. Trust us.
jokes.setup: Britta giving an unexpectedly good political speech
jokes.punchline: Jeff cutting off her momentum when she gets too real
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeff's discomfort with real political discourse.

jokes.text: Cancel the show and you cancel people. Might as well bring dinosaurs back.
jokes.setup: Britta's passionate defense of free speech
jokes.punchline: Connects censorship to bringing back dinosaurs through unclear logic
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta non-sequitur - her passion leads to confusing metaphors. Very character-appropriate but structurally weak

jokes.text: I kinda zoned out and assume we're standing to go see Avengers.
jokes.setup: Group stands up in solidarity for free speech
jokes.punchline: Reveals one person wasn't paying attention and made wrong assumption
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment - callbacks to earlier Avengers discussion for added effect

jokes.text: No matter what happens I'm with you, but don't tell anyone I'm with you. If you do, I'll deny it, but I'm with you here and now. Not literally here and now, right now I'm leaving, but I will always have been with you secretly.
jokes.setup: Chang expressing support for the group's stand
jokes.punchline: Increasingly contradictory qualifications that negate his support while claiming it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang - his cowardly self-interest disguised as support. Character integration is excellent, though could be more economical

jokes.text: Case in point, am I right? Definitely get my good side, right here.
jokes.setup: Making a speech about free speech being offensive
jokes.punchline: Immediately focuses on looking good on camera instead of the principle
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing vanity undermining principled stance - typical of the show's cynicism about motives

jokes.text: What do we say? No peeking at the leaking. Promise, promise, promise?
jokes.setup: Making everyone promise not to look at leaked emails
jokes.punchline: Childish rhyme followed by over-eager prompting for promises
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyme that feels forced - functional but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Well, you turned into the path of least resistance.
jokes.setup: Annie thanking Jeff for backing her up
jokes.punchline: Jeff's cynical reframing of his support as self-serving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff - his inability to accept credit for doing the right thing, always finding the cynical angle

jokes.text: It's not like I used that stupid Greendale email for anything but porn subscriptions and improv show mailing lists.
jokes.setup: Jeff downplaying risk of email leak
jokes.punchline: Reveals embarrassing but relatively harmless email uses
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Jeff's vanity paired with admission of mundane internet habits

jokes.text: They got it. They got everything. It's Vietnam now, baby. It's Vietnam!
jokes.setup: Realization that hackers have access to all emails, not just Greendale
jokes.punchline: Escalates to dramatic war metaphor with repeated Vietnam reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurd war metaphor - the repetition and dramatic delivery sell the panic

jokes.text: Girls. Frankie. Francesca.
jokes.setup: Tense greeting between characters after email leak
jokes.punchline: Overly formal, awkward greeting style suggesting discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good awkward tension moment - the formality suggests everyone knows something but won't admit it

jokes.text: Something was wrong with that one. Bad chair.
jokes.setup: Presumably someone has trouble with a chair while setting up
jokes.punchline: Blames the chair rather than admitting clumsiness
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy deflection - functional but not particularly creative or memorable

jokes.text: I'd hate to think my wardrobe wasn't passing muster with this group. So here I am in a brand new outfit with an up-to-the-minute shirt.
jokes.setup: Character responding to compliment about 'new look'
jokes.punchline: Overly defensive response revealing insecurity about appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character insecurity moment - the overly defensive response reveals they read criticism about their clothes

jokes.text: I have read everything you guys have ever wrote about me in an email. And you guys are the worst people in the world.
jokes.setup: Abed admits to reading all the leaked emails
jokes.punchline: Delivers devastating judgment with his characteristic blunt honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment - his analytical nature leads to brutal but accurate assessment. Character integration is exceptional

jokes.text: I will bite your little finger off and I will eat it! Then, let's see you type more descriptions of my Houseguest-era Sinbad wardrobe.
jokes.setup: Character getting angry about email descriptions
jokes.punchline: Violent threat followed by very specific reference to 1990s Sinbad fashion comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from violence to very specific pop culture insult - 'Houseguest-era Sinbad' is brilliantly specific

jokes.text: You had my blood tested for amphetamines? You were extra jumpy last spring. We had to double check to be safe.
jokes.setup: Annie discovers friends secretly tested her blood
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact justification for invasive medical testing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation revealing how paranoid and invasive the group can be - casual justification makes it funnier

jokes.text: For that human genome project? We only used half of it for that. By the way, you're one percent Neanderthal.
jokes.setup: Annie gave blood for what she thought was a legitimate science project
jokes.punchline: Reveals they lied about the purpose and casually delivers genetic results
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - reveals the deception then casually drops genetic results as afterthought

jokes.text: Unless you're too busy telling your girlfriend I don't understand Donnie Darko.
jokes.setup: Annie asks Abed to help with sound
jokes.punchline: Abed's hurt about having his film expertise questioned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed wound - questioning his understanding of a cult film hits at his core identity as the group's film expert

jokes.text: Oh, or will me opening a box office unfairly influence wagers in your betting pool about my sexual preference?
jokes.setup: Volunteer to set up box office
jokes.punchline: Reveals knowledge of betting pool about their sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing another layer of group betrayal - the betting pool is a nice escalation of invasiveness

jokes.text: You told human resources I was a functional alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Frankie confronting Britta about the leaked emails
jokes.punchline: Revealing Britta reported her drinking to HR
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the professional consequences of the group's gossiping.

jokes.text: What in your unqualified, buzzword-bloated, little red school house of a brain is a functional alcoholic?
jokes.setup: Angry about being called a functional alcoholic
jokes.punchline: Elaborate, creative insult questioning the term while revealing anger
jokes.explanation: Excellent creative insult - 'buzzword-bloated, little red school house of a brain' is original and character-specific

jokes.text: What in your brain is a chapstick lesbian? To me, 300 bucks.
jokes.setup: Counter-attack about sexual betting pool
jokes.punchline: Reveals specific bet amount while questioning the term
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - the specific dollar amount makes the betting pool feel more real and invasive

jokes.text: How does the tigers survive without food or water? Oh, it's not cute, Annie.
jokes.setup: Metaphor about being judged on a tiger-infested island with no food or water
jokes.punchline: Annie's logical question about tiger survival ruins the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie moment - her analytical nature ruins dramatic metaphors. Great character integration

jokes.text: She's dead. I pretend to write her emails as a journaling device.
jokes.setup: Criticism about sister never responding to emails
jokes.punchline: Reveals sister is dead, then explains he knows this and does it anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - perfect setup/reveal structure with Jeff's characteristic emotional detachment making it even funnier

jokes.text: She's dead.
jokes.setup: Accused of writing long emails to sister who doesn't respond
jokes.punchline: Reveals sister is dead, making the criticism cruel
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy beat - the economy of the reveal and the complete shift in emotional tone is perfectly executed

jokes.text: You wretched, evasive little gremlin.
jokes.setup: Annie's deflection of the dead sister revelation
jokes.punchline: Frankie's elaborate and oddly specific insult
jokes.explanation: Memorably specific insult - 'wretched, evasive little gremlin' has great rhythm and specificity

jokes.text: I received their first letter as part of a family email chain, Christmas of 2007. I responded politely. When I realized they had somehow mistaken me for their cousin... I simply failed to disabuse them of that notion.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his relationship with the Patterson family
jokes.punchline: Admits to maintaining a years-long deception for companionship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff pathology - his loneliness leading to elaborate deception. 'Failed to disabuse' is perfectly formal Jeff language

jokes.text: 3D models you've been making of our bodies without our permission. That's what those photos were for? I'm making a game about lady time travelers. Where in your pants?
jokes.setup: Revelation about Elroy taking photos for 3D models
jokes.punchline: His innocent game explanation followed by crude assumption about purpose
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from innocent to creepy back to crude - shows how paranoid everyone has become

jokes.text: Except for February 7th, 2013. Chang, Britta is one, Annie is two. Jeff, ha, yeah. Jeff, gross.
jokes.setup: Jeff denying participation in ranking emails
jokes.punchline: Specific date and response proving he did participate
jokes.explanation: Great callback with specific date - Jeff's hypocrisy exposed with perfect evidence including his typical reactions

jokes.text: Hey, silver and gold, ladies, ain't no losers there.
jokes.setup: Awkward revelation about ranking the women
jokes.punchline: Chang's attempt to spin misogyny as complimentary
jokes.explanation: Typical Chang damage control - his attempt to make sexist ranking sound positive is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Jezel is like a daughter to me. But arguably more like a daughter to her parents.
jokes.setup: Defense of not making inappropriate models of younger character
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the obvious while defending his position
jokes.explanation: Perfect Elroy deadpan - acknowledges the obvious logic while maintaining his weird position

jokes.text: Oh, and by the way, clearly, not a life coach, and absolutely, just an Italian sociopath you met at a dispensary.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Britta's life coach emails
jokes.punchline: Jeff's brutal assessment of Britta's 'professional' help
jokes.explanation: 'Italian sociopath you met at a dispensary' is a perfect encapsulation of Britta's poor judgment in seeking help

jokes.text: Yeah, Chang was our teacher. What? That's right, and frankly, haven't been well utilized since.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Chang used to be their teacher
jokes.punchline: Chang's complaint about not being properly used since then
jokes.explanation: Good Chang moment - his narcissistic complaint about not being central enough to the group dynamics

jokes.text: Dear new ensemble, I love you more than my dad. What!
jokes.setup: Reading embarrassing emails aloud
jokes.punchline: Overly emotional declaration followed by shocked reaction to it being read
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - the overly emotional email content followed by embarrassment at exposure

jokes.text: Dear new ensemble, I love you more than my dad.
jokes.setup: Reading Jeff's embarrassing astronaut letters
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confession of loving the group more than family
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character revelation - Jeff's emotional honesty about the group being his real family is both funny and touching

jokes.text: This is the first school in six months that hasn't canceled, so thanks.
jokes.setup: Comedian arrives during group meltdown
jokes.punchline: Reveals their 'principled stand' is just the only booking he could get
jokes.explanation: Good ironic deflation - their moral stand is revealed to be practical desperation, undercutting their righteousness

jokes.text: You can stop me, but you can't stop the people from stopping this show.
jokes.setup: Neil trying to leave to avoid performing
jokes.punchline: Logical but convoluted statement about the mob coming to stop the show
jokes.explanation: Good logical twist - Neil trying to use the mob's arrival as justification for his cowardice

jokes.text: Listen, you piece of crap, we've been to hell and back for you. Get up there and do your stupid act for freedom.
jokes.setup: Trying to convince reluctant comedian to perform
jokes.punchline: Inspirational speech that's simultaneously insulting and patriotic
jokes.explanation: Excellent contradiction - 'piece of crap' and 'for freedom' in same breath perfectly captures the absurd situation

jokes.text: Look, I see there's a lot of Jews in the crowd. I mean, you know, there isn't, but usually when I do my act, there's more.
jokes.setup: Comedian starting his racist routine
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself about crowd composition
jokes.explanation: Shows the comedian is genuinely bad at his job - can't even read his audience correctly

jokes.text: Look, I see there's a lot of Jews in the crowd. I mean, you know, there isn't, but usually when I do my act, there's more.
jokes.setup: Comedian starting his racist routine
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging there are no Jews present but continuing the bit anyway
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment that shows the absurdity of doing offensive material without targets.

jokes.text: when you're black, your community steals all your food, so you can't eat as much. And when you're Jewish, nobody ever buys anything, so you can't eat as much.
jokes.setup: Comedian explaining why it's hard to be fat when you're black or Jewish
jokes.punchline: Delivers lazy racist stereotypes as punchlines
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad comedy - lazy stereotypes meant to show how awful the comedian is. Low craft scores are intentional

jokes.text: Hey, Leonard, you enjoy my girlfriend in 2009? As much as she enjoyed your plagiarized poetry.
jokes.setup: Audience member confronts Leonard about something in leaked emails
jokes.punchline: Leonard's perfect comeback revealing his own dirt on the accuser
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how the email leak has created mutual assured destruction - Leonard's comeback is perfect

jokes.text: Man, it's like Road Warrior out there, but if petrol were privacy and bad teeth were dirty secrets.
jokes.setup: Describing the chaos outside
jokes.punchline: Extended Mad Max metaphor with specific substitutions
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor that works well - specific substitutions make it feel crafted rather than random

jokes.text: This is Ryan, AKA StringCheeseHustler, AKA Fartmitzvah, Skullandbones88, Frozensucked, Captaintitty. You don't need to know all his names.
jokes.setup: Introducing the arrested hacker
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous usernames followed by self-aware cutoff
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd usernames with self-aware ending - captures the juvenile nature of internet trolls

jokes.text: No one's free when they're one of anything.
jokes.setup: Ryan says he was 'one of us' and 'free'
jokes.punchline: Warburton's pseudo-profound response about group identity
jokes.explanation: Nicely crafted pseudo-philosophical line that sounds profound but is actually meaningless - very quotable

jokes.text: Common courtesy is just gibberish, hocus pocus, a spell we cast when hoping to cheat reality.
jokes.setup: Question about common courtesy
jokes.punchline: Cynical philosophical dismissal of social niceties as magical thinking
jokes.explanation: Good cynical observation with poetic language - 'spell we cast when hoping to cheat reality' is well-crafted

jokes.text: Yankee Doodle Dandy!
jokes.setup: Asked how fast he can get to mainframe
jokes.punchline: Completely random patriotic exclamation
jokes.explanation: Random patriotic outburst fits Warburton's eccentric character but feels somewhat arbitrary as an ending beat

tmdb_overview: A hacker angry over a racist comic's planned campus performance threatens to publish the activity committee's personal emails.
insight: Season 6's softest outing: 70.8 score masks weak joke impact despite steady density.
